english: I. pf. bčol (bcol), fut. γžol (gzhol) (?) 1. to entrust a person with a thing, to commit a thing to another's charge; to make, appoint, dé-la rgyál-po ₀čól-lo (de la rgyal po 'chol lo) they made him king Pth.; btsún-mo-la rtá-rdzi bčól-lo (btsun mo la rta rdzi bcol lo) they made the queen tend the horses Glr.; tab-γóg ₀čól žig (thab g.yog 'chol zhig) he may be employed as a kitchenboy, scullion Pth.; dbaṅ-méd-du ₀čól-ba (dbang med du 'chol ba) to make one powerless, to compel by authority Glr.; bčól-bai γnyer (bcol ba'i gnyer) Lex. manager; ₀čól-bai ṅo ('chol ba'i ngo) Lex. intercessor; pi-wáṅ-la ram-₀dégs bčól-nas glu blaṅs (pi wang la ram 'degs bcol nas glu blangs) she sang with accompaniment of the guitar (lit. committing the accompaniment to the guitar) Glr.; *kyab čól-la* (skyabs bcol la) (for ₀čol-du ('chol du)) *yoṅ-če* (yong ce) W. to place one's self under another man's protection. -- 2. to commit, commend, recommend, lás ₀čól-ba (las 'chol ba) to commission one with an affair or transaction; resp. prín (-las) ₀čól-ba ('phrin (-las) 'chol ba), though prín ('phrin) (-las) seems to be sometimes a mere pleonasm: báṅ-so yul deï lha-srúṅ-rnams-la prín-bčól mdzád-do (bang so yul de'i lha srung rnams la 'phrin bcol mdzad do) (the king) recommended the sepulchre to the tutelar gods of the country Glr.; *čól-te bór-če* (bcol te 'bor ce) W. to deposit a thing for temporary keeping. II. = ₀čál-ba ('chal ba) 1. Cs. to change, to turn aside (?) -- 2. to be thrown together confusedly, e.g. of the loose leaves of a (Tibetan) book; ₀čól-bar byéd-pa ('chol bar byed pa) to put in disorder, to confuse, to confound Ma.; dge-sdíg ₀čól-bar ₀gro (dge sdig 'chol bar 'gro) virtue and vice are confounded Ma.; *i lé-ka čól dug* (i le ka 'chol dug) W. this affair goes wrong, turns out badly; in a special sense: to rave, to be delirious C.; *čol-láb gyáb-pa* (chol lab rgyab pa) C. id.; *nyid-čol láb-pa, gyag-pa* (gnyid chol lab pa, rgyag pa) C., to talk confusedly whilst being heavy with sleep; *čól-k̃a* ('chol kha) C. senseless talk; *čól-k̃an-ni ṭú-gu, čol-ṭúg* ('chol mkhan ni phru gu, chol phrug) W. being of a mixed race; illegitimate or bastard child, bastard. -- 3. morally: to break a vow; *a-ne čol soṅ* (a ne chol song) he has broken his vow on account of a woman, i.e. by having married.
Cs.: 1. a thing committed to another's care. -- 2. a sly, crafty woman, Sch. a dissolute woman.

I. pf. bčos (bcos) or ₀čos ('chos), fut. bčo (bco), imp. čos (chos), supine bčós-su (bcos su) Dzl. 2, 4, W. *čò-če* (bco ce), pf. and imp. *čos* (bcos), to make, make ready, prepare, to construct, build, a bow, a road etc. Glr.; ₀čós-sam ('chos sam) am I to build? Glr.; drés-ma tág-par ₀čós-pa (dres ma thag par 'chos pa) to make ropes out of drésma (dres ma) (a kind of grass) prop. to work drésma (dres ma) into ropes, Glr.; γzab ₀čós-pa (gzab 'chos pa) to adjust one's ornaments Sch.; lus ₀čós-pa (lus 'chos pa) to dress, to trim one's self up Sch.; γsár-du ₀čós-pa (gsar du 'chos pa) to renew, renovate, repair Sch.; ltúṅ-ba pyir ₀čós-pa (ltung ba phyir 'chos pa) Tar. 95, 20 perh. to retouch, amend, correct, improve. -- tsúl-₀čos (tshul 'chos) hypocrisy, a mere outward performance of religious rites and observances Mil., tsul-₀čos ma byas spyód-pa (tshul 'chos ma byas spyod pa) to live without hypocrisy Mil.; tsúl-₀čos-mk̃an (tshul 'chos mkhan) hypocrite. -- tsúl-₀čos-pa (tshul 'chos pa) or bčós-pa (bcos pa) acc. to Cs. also an established rule or canon. II. Sch.: to gnaw off (secondary form to ₀čá-ba ('cha' ba)).

1. the letter ǰ (j), media, palatal, like the Italian gi in Giovanni, g in giro; in C. as initial deep-sounding and aspirated, ǰh (jh). -- 2. numerical figure: 7. -- 3. tea, resp. γsol-ǰa (gsol ja). For the trade in Central Asia it is pressed into brick-shaped lumps, a portion of which, when to be used, is pulverized and boiled, having been well compounded with butter and salt or soda (bul (bul)) by means of a kind of churn of bamboo (gur-gúr (gur gur)), after which it is drunk as hot as possible.
